x-enumeration-literals.name: Direct Balance Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The redeemed value is applied directly to an account balance.

Comment:
Example: Cashback added to a current account balance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Statement Offset Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The value is used to reduce an outstanding statement or liability.

Comment:
Example: Rewards applied to reduce a credit card bill.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Transaction-Specific Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption value is applied to a particular purchase or transaction chosen by the customer.

Comment:
Example: Using points to pay for a specific online purchase.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The redemption must be used entirely at once for a single purpose.

Comment:
Example: A €50 voucher must be spent in one transaction.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redeemed value can be applied partially and the remaining balance can be saved for later use.

Comment:
Example: Redeeming 500 points for €5 now, keeping the rest for future use.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Split Allocation Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redeemed rewards are distributed across multiple targets or uses simultaneously.

Comment:
Example: Half of points applied to a bill, half converted into a voucher.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Priority-Based Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption is applied following a predefined priority sequence.

Comment:
Example: Applied first to fees, then interest, then principal.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption is applied only if certain conditions are met.

Comment:
Example: Cashback applied only if a minimum purchase amount is reached.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Merchant Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption can be used only at selected merchants or partner networks.

Comment:
Example: Loyalty points usable only at partner stores.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Automatic Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption is applied automatically by the system without customer intervention.

Comment:
Example: Rewards automatically credited to a statement at month-end.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Manual Selection Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The customer chooses how and where to apply the redeemed rewards.

Comment:
Example: Selecting between cash, voucher, or donation options.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Scheduled Redemption Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption is applied at a later time or specific scheduled event.

Comment:
Example: Points redeemed now but applied to the next billing cycle.
